MuhammadHaris

My 5-year journey from casual gamer to CEO. This portfolio documents every step, failure, and breakthrough along the way.

Timeline

My Journey

From 2020 to 2026 — how I went from gaming to building a tech company.

2020

Gaming Background

Played competitive games like Age of Empires, Free Fire, King of Fighters. Good at gaming but realized it wasn't my long-term path.

2021

Content Creation

Started YouTube channel creating gaming and tech content. Learned video editing, storytelling, and audience building. First step into the digital world.

2022

Tech Transition

Shifted focus from gaming to tech. Started exploring web development and programming. Left gaming behind to build real skills.

2023

Web Development

Completed Coursera and Codecademy courses. Built my first websites, learned HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React. Countless late nights debugging code.

2024

Harvard CS50 & Security

Completed Harvard CS50 (Programming with Python). Started cybersecurity on TryHackMe. Deep dive into Kali Linux and penetration testing.

2025

Certifications & AI

Earned TryHackMe certifications. Built PrecisionFlow.io - my first AI agent. Founded PrecisionTechInsights. Explored crypto trading.

2026

CEO at Itovio

Launched Itovio.com as the commercial brand. Leading a team of specialists. Completed LinkedIn Learning certificates. Shifted from solo freelancer to CEO.

Portfolio

What I've Built

Current Role

CEO at Itovio.com

Leading a team delivering end-to-end IT solutions. My current focus and the culmination of my 5-year journey.

Visit
AI Product

PrecisionFlow.io

Autonomous AI agent I built that generates daily crypto content for Binance Square — my first successful AI automation.

Visit
Company

PrecisionTechInsights

The parent company I founded. Started as a solo venture, now evolved into Itovio with a full team.

Visit
Personal Brand

LinkedIn Journey

Built 5K+ followers sharing my learning journey. Documented every step from gaming to CEO.

Visit
Security Profile

TryHackMe - Top 2%

Top 2% hacker on TryHackMe with 34 badges, 226 rooms completed, and 5 recognized premium certificates. Active in cybersecurity challenges.

Visit
Development

GitHub Profile

Active developer building guides and projects. Many repos are private, but I share useful tools and learning resources publicly.

Visit
